friction_items.description: tool_audit_synthesis STILL applies IMRAD expectations (abstract, methods, results, discussion) to a humanities essay even after sys_config_set domain=humanities. The humanities essay scaffold writes introduction/contextual_framing/close_reading_one/close_reading_two/close_reading_three/critical_conversation/counter_argument_and_reply/conclusion_and_stakes — none of which the audit recognises. Audit reports missing_sections=[abstract, methods, results, discussion], short_sections covering all five, and 5 gate_blockers. This is the exact same baseline HIGH friction: the audit gate is implicitly STEM-PDF-shaped and ignores domain.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make tool_audit_synthesis read researcher_config.domain. For humanities, expect the 8-section humanities-essay shape produced by tool_humanities_essay_scaffold, not IMRAD. Mirror the existing humanities_essay venue template logic.

friction_items.description: Project Gutenberg STILL missing from tool_humanities_archive_lookup, and now strictly worse than baseline. Passing archives=['gutenberg'] is silently accepted at the tool layer (returns status=success with archives_planned=['gutenberg']), but the written plan file at inputs/archival/lookup_<q>.md contains the line '_Unknown archive — skipped._' under the ## gutenberg heading. A naive AI seeing success in the JSON has no signal that the actual plan is empty. Either reject unknown archives at the input layer or add Gutenberg as a real provider.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add 'gutenberg' to the archives enum + handler with endpoint https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/search/?query={q}. Until then, the tool should return status=error / warnings list naming the skipped archives, not silently accept and skip.

friction_items.description: close_reading.yaml line 94 STILL references 'tool_humanities_apparatus_audit (planned; see docs/ROADMAP.md)'. The tool does NOT exist in TOOL_DEFINITIONS or _HANDLERS. Shipping a 'planned' tool reference inside a stable v2.0 protocol is the same v2.0 blocker called out in the baseline. A naive AI reading the step will try to call it and hit a tool-not-found.
friction_items.suggested_fix: For v2.0.0, either implement tool_humanities_apparatus_audit (the humanities pack needs it anyway) or strip the conditional sentence from close_reading.yaml step declare_step_contract. Currently the fallback ('manual review against quality_bar') is buried beneath the planned-tool sentence.

friction_items.description: Three router tools still present: tool_route, tool_quick_route, tool_semantic_route. sys_help and the new MCP instructions field only name tool_route in the boot sequence ('(2) call tool_route(prompt=<user_message>)'). The other two are dead weight on the catalog from the AI's POV — naive AI won't know to use them, but they consume tokens at list_tools and dilute the routing-tool choice.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either fold tool_quick_route detection inline into tool_route (return is_quick=true when matched) and drop the standalone, or use the v2.0 MAJOR window to consolidate to a single tool_route with mode parameter.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_synthesis raised KeyError('paper_path') when called without paper_path. The error returned to the AI was the bare string "'paper_path'". A required-field validator would have produced a clearer message ('missing required arg paper_path'). Same risk on any other audit tool that crashes inside the handler before validating arguments.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Have the dispatch layer validate required fields against inputSchema['required'] BEFORE calling the handler. Return a structured 'missing_required_args: [paper_path]' error.

friction_items.description: sys_protocol_get takes 'protocol_name' but the natural AI guess is 'protocol' (matching how tool_route returns 'primary_protocol'). First call returned a confusing error message: 'argument of type NoneType is not iterable'. The schema field name should match the natural reference. Either rename the parameter to 'protocol' (no breakage; old isn't in any v1.x project) or accept both via alias.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Rename inputSchema field to 'protocol' OR accept both 'protocol' and 'protocol_name' in the handler. Definitely improve the missing-required-arg error path (same root as the tool_audit_synthesis case).

friction_items.description: Protocol summary format is improved (~4.4K chars and returns step_summary as id+name only — real progress). BUT the full prose descriptions still load on format=step or format=full, and no protocol has the concise_steps: block recommended in the baseline. AI consuming a single step still gets 8-15 lines of literary-critical prose. The summary mode helps planning; concise_steps would help execution.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add concise_steps: [imperative verbs] to each protocol YAML; have format=step return the concise form unless verbose=true is passed. Generates ~10-line bundles from existing steps[].name.

friction_items.description: tool_route returned decomposition (4-tool sequence) but no chain_preview showing the protocol arc (close_reading -> citation_chains -> humanities_essay_structure -> synthesis_paper). The 'why' field calls out the top intent_class only. A naive AI sees the immediate decomposition but not the multi-protocol future. recommended_action field IS present (one improvement), but it only names the NEXT tool, not the chain.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add chain_preview: [list of protocol_ids] to the tool_route response. Compute from each protocol's next_protocol field at startup.

friction_items.description: Calling tool_audit_step_completeness with step_id='declare_step_contract' (taken straight from sys_protocol_get summary output of close_reading) returned 'Step "declare_step_contract" not found.' The audit looks at workspace/<step>/ folder names, not protocol step ids. Naive AI is led to assume step_id is a primary key shared across tools; in fact it's a path lookup.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Document the expected workspace shape in the step_id description, OR have the audit accept both protocol-step-id and folder-name forms. Returning the actually-available step folder list in the error would also help.

friction_items.description: Humanities pack still has 4 tools (essay_scaffold, archive_lookup, transcribe, citation_chain) for 8 protocols. close_reading, distant_reading, hermeneutic_method, digital_humanities_workflow, scholarly_edition still have NO pack-specific tool. Baseline complaint preserved. The pack-leverage gap vs wet_lab / engineering is unchanged.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add at minimum tool_humanities_annotation_scaffold (writes the line-anchor annotation file template) and tool_humanities_apparatus_audit (implements the protocol-promised audit). Scaffolds, not full pipelines, are 80% of the leverage.

friction_items.description: After tool_humanities_essay_scaffold + paper compile, no workspace/<step>/ folders exist. tool_audit_quality_full reports 'All active steps pass the completeness gate' because the active-steps list is empty. A naive AI following close_reading's instruction to write step_summary.yaml has no way to know which folder it goes in (declare_step_contract -> workspace/declare_step_contract/step_summary.yaml? or workspace/<step_index>/?). The protocol doctrine assumes the AI knows the path convention.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Have sys_protocol_get format=step return the expected workspace path for that step, OR have the protocol loader auto-create workspace/<step_id>/ stubs.

friction_items.description: 146 MCP-exposed tools is much better than 198+, but for the humanities close_reading scenario only ~14 are actually used (sys_boot, tool_route, sys_protocol_get, sys_state_get, sys_config_set, tool_humanities_essay_scaffold, tool_humanities_archive_lookup, tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_quality_full, tool_paper_compile_typst, sys_file_write/read/list, mem_log). The other 132 are still visible noise during the AI's catalog scan. Domain packs being live=146/146 (no on-demand loading visible to the AI) means the AI still sees slurm/snakemake/nextflow tools even though they will never trigger on a humanities prompt.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Honor 'Pack tools are loaded on-demand via tool_route routing' (per MCP instructions field) by actually hiding non-core packs from list_tools until tool_route activates them. Today the field claims on-demand, but the catalog is flat.

improvements_vs_baseline: Tool catalog 198+ -> 146 (-26%): a real reduction the AI sees on list_tools.

Audit cluster 24+ -> 4 (tool_audit, tool_audit_findings, tool_audit_quality_full, tool_discussion_coverage_audit): the single biggest consolidation win, directly addresses the baseline 'figure/dashboard audit cluster' complaint.
Synthesis/figure/dashboard cluster 18 -> 6: tool_figure and tool_dashboard subsume the 14+ previous wrappers.
Search consolidated 5 -> 1 (tool_search): no deprecated provider-specific aliases visible in TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
status field present on 146/146 tools (all 'live' — no alias/deprecated noise in the catalog).
pack field present on 146/146 tools (core: 123; humanities/qualitative/theory_math/wet_lab/engineering each 3; slurm/snakemake/nextflow/cytoscape/redcap/synapse).
scope_tags present on protocols (verified on humanities/textual/close_reading and synthesis/humanities_essay_structure).
sys_protocol_get default format=summary (~4.4K chars, returns step_summary as id+name only — was full body in baseline, now planning-shaped).
MCP server instructions field names the boot sequence ('sys_boot -> tool_route -> sys_protocol_get format=summary -> sys_active_tools') — the naive AI sees this on init.

tool_route returns recommended_action field (concrete next call), why_matched field, active_tools whitelist (17 tools scoped instead of 198 to scan), decomposition (4-tool plan in one shot), semantic_candidates with scores, and ambiguous_alternatives.

Final deliverable (synthesis/paper.pdf, 5 pages, 50KB) compiles cleanly via tool_paper_compile_typst venue=humanities_essay — drafter_loop ran 3 iterations and produced a valid PDF without manual intervention.
All 4 turns completed with a sub-20-tool surface, no dead-end traversal, and no tool that needed to be 'discovered' outside the tool_route decomposition.
Server modularization (Phase 10): import surface is research_os.server with dispatch.py, handlers/, entry.py — easier for future debugging.
remaining_recommendations: Make audit gates domain-aware in v2.0.0 BEFORE the release: tool_audit_synthesis must read researcher_config.domain and apply the humanities essay shape (not IMRAD) when domain=humanities. This is the highest-impact remaining HIGH friction — the audit silently STEM-shames every humanities project.

Add Gutenberg to tool_humanities_archive_lookup as a first-class archive, OR have the tool reject unknown archives at the input layer instead of silently dropping them in the written plan. The current silent-skip is a v2.0 regression vs baseline (baseline said 'not supported'; v2 says 'success' then drops it).
Remove or implement tool_humanities_apparatus_audit before v2.0.0 ships. Shipping a 'planned' tool reference in close_reading.yaml is a v2.0 blocker per the baseline; it is still there.

Fold tool_quick_route into tool_route (or use the v2.0 MAJOR window to drop it). MCP instructions only mention tool_route — having three router tools in the catalog is preserved cognitive load for no naive-AI benefit.

Validate required arguments at the dispatch layer using inputSchema.required; return structured 'missing_required_args: [...]' instead of raw KeyError. tool_audit_synthesis returned the bare string 'paper_path' as the error message.

Rename sys_protocol_get's 'protocol_name' to 'protocol' (or accept both via alias). 'protocol' matches what tool_route returns as primary_protocol; naive AI's first guess fails today with 'argument of type NoneType is not iterable'.

Add concise_steps: blocks to every protocol YAML for AI consumption (preserves the rich literary-critical descriptions for human readers). Biggest remaining token-reduction lever; baseline ask, not implemented.

Add chain_preview field to tool_route output naming the multi-protocol arc (close_reading -> citation_chains -> humanities_essay_structure -> synthesis_paper). recommended_action covers the next single tool; chain_preview would cover the next 3-4 protocols.
Add at least tool_humanities_annotation_scaffold (line-anchor file template) to fill the humanities pack's 4-tools-for-8-protocols gap. Scaffolds (not pipelines) are sufficient.

Honor the MCP-instructions claim of 'Pack tools loaded on-demand via tool_route routing' — today the catalog is flat at 146 tools and the AI sees slurm/snakemake/nextflow regardless of intent. Hide non-core packs from list_tools until tool_route activates the pack.
